fact: February 15 is exactly 46 days into the year, which means you've already lived through more than 12% of the entire year!
In the Northern Hemisphere, this is typically prime time for maple trees to start their sap runs - the sweet stuff that becomes maple syrup is often flowing like crazy right now!
Around this time, the soil temperature often hits that perfect sweet spot where hibernating animals start stirring but it's still too cold to fully wake up.
February 15 falls in the middle of Aquarius season, making it one of the most 'air sign' days of the year according to astrology.
In Antarctica, this date marks peak summer - penguins are having their warmest weather while the Northern Hemisphere is bundled up in winter!
The sun typically rises about 2 minutes earlier each day around February 15 in northern places, adding up to nearly 20 extra minutes of daylight each week.
Wolves in cold regions are often at their hungriest right now because their winter fat stores are running low, but spring prey hasn't appeared yet.
February 15 sits exactly 33 days before the spring equinox, making it nature's 'pre-game show' time in northern climates.
In many temperate places, this is when you'll see the weirdest weather - it might snow in the morning and feel like spring by afternoon because seasons are basically arm-wrestling.
Earthworms often start their upward journey around this date in temperate zones, even though the ground is still cold - they can sense spring coming from deep underground!
When there's a full moon on February 15, it's called a 'Snow Moon' and appears extra bright because of all the white snow reflecting light in northern regions.
In the Southern Hemisphere, February 15 is peak summer time when the sun sets super late and everything feels lazy and hot.
